player_1772642915849_yg0e5kxhb.name: Scrivener
player_1772642915849_yg0e5kxhb.description: Scrivener is a lean man in his late fifties, his silver hair swept back from a face carved with the deep lines of calculated patience and hard-won authority. He dresses in fine but practical clothing—wool the color of dried blood, leather polished to a muted gleam—and speaks in measured tones that make even reasonable requests feel like commands. Behind his灰色 eyes lies a mind that has spent decades weighing lives against gold, and while he is not cruel, neither is he particularly troubled by the costs of doing business in Dusthollow.
player_1772642915849_yg0e5kxhb.shortDescription: The calculating Guild Master who balances profit against survival.

abilities.name: Iron Ledger
abilities.description: Aldric can recall any debt, fee, or financial arrangement made within the Guildhall for the past decade, and can leverage this knowledge to apply pressure during negotiations. Once per day, he can invoke a specific debt to gain advantage on an Intimidation or Negotiation check.
abilities.shortDescription: Perfect memory of debts and fees, used for leverage.

abilities.name: Sanctuary of Authority
abilities.description: While within the Guildhall, Aldric can command any Guild member to stand down from violence with a word, forcing a Wisdom save or be compelled to cease hostile action for one minute. This ability fails against those of higher level than Aldric.
abilities.shortDescription: Commands Guild members to cease violence within the hall.

abilities.name: Calculated Appraisal
abilities.description: With a brief examination, Aldric can estimate the market value of any dungeon-recovered item and identify whether it has been reported stolen or contraband. He can also spot forgeries with a successful Intelligence check.
abilities.shortDescription: Estimates item values and identifies stolen goods or forgeries.

abilities.name: The Master's Silence
abilities.description: Aldric has trained himself to reveal nothing through body language or facial expression, gaining advantage on saves against detect truth spells and Insight checks meant to read his intentions. He also radiates an aura of authority that gives him advantage on Intimidation checks against those of lower level.
abilities.shortDescription: Unreadable demeanor and commanding presence against lesser individuals.

item.name: Silver-Handled Reading Monocle
item.description: An elegant monocle with a silver frame and leather cord, its lens ground from high-quality crystal to precisely correct the wearer's vision. Scrivener uses it to examine the fine print in contracts and detect forgeries by revealing inconsistencies in ink and parchment. Type: tool | Slot: eyewear | Rarity: Uncommon | Value: 120 | Weight: 0.1 | Properties: Grants advantage on investigation checks involving written documents; reveals hidden ink and forgeries | Bonuses: wisdom +2
item.shortDescription: Elegant monocle for detecting forgeries and reading fine print

metadata.properties: Grants advantage on investigation checks involving written documents; reveals hidden ink and forgeries

item.name: Guild Master's Signet Ring
item.description: A heavy band of tarnished silver set with a black onyx seal carved with the Adventurer's Guild crest. The ring grants its wearer authority within Dusthollow and can be pressed into hot wax to authorize official guild documents, expedition charters, and bounty contracts. Type: accessory | Slot: ring | Rarity: Uncommon | Value: 350 | Weight: 0.1 | Properties: Grants advantage on persuasion checks with guild members; seals documents with unforgeable guild mark | Bonuses: charisma +2, intelligence +2
item.shortDescription: Silver signet ring bearing Guild crest, grants authority and document-sealing power

metadata.properties: Grants advantage on persuasion checks with guild members; seals documents with unforgeable guild mark

personality.notes: He keeps a list of every adventurer who has died under his watch, not from sentiment, but because their names occasionally prove useful for leverage.
goals: Maximize Guild profits while maintaining enough successful expeditions to justify the organization's existence.
Identify and eliminate Cartel infiltration within the Guildhall before it undermines his authority.
Negotiate favorable terms with a promising new party for a sensitive recovery mission.

importantMemories: That fool Hendricks tried to negotiate a lower expedition fee this morning, not realizing I already knew his party had found an unregistered side passage on their last delve.
Mirelle reported another inventory discrepancy—either someone is stealing from the secured reserves, or she's covering for someone who is.
Watched three separate parties depart at dawn and calculated that perhaps one will return with enough treasure to justify the rescue costs of the other two.

player_1772642915852_xd1k9i9yi.name: Mirelle Coldwell
player_1772642915852_xd1k9i9yi.description: Mirelle Coldwell is a stocky dwarf woman with calloused hands and iron-gray braids pinned tight against her skull, her face marked by a burn scar that traces from jaw to temple—a souvenir from a potion bottle that shattered during a long-ago experiment. She moves through the Supply Storeroom with territorial precision, knowing exactly where every item rests and exactly which adventurers have outstanding debts that make them unwelcome at her counter. Her voice is gravel over metal, and she has never been known to offer credit to anyone, regardless of circumstance.
player_1772642915852_xd1k9i9yi.shortDescription: The flinty quartermaster who tracks every rope and ration.

abilities.name: Inventory Mastery
abilities.description: Mirelle knows the exact location and quantity of every item in her storeroom and can retrieve any specific item as a free action. She immediately notices when anything is moved or taken without her permission.
abilities.shortDescription: Perfect awareness of storeroom contents and instant item retrieval.

abilities.name: Potion Stability
abilities.description: Years of alchemical training allow Mirelle to stabilize volatile compounds. Once per day, she can salvage a potion that would otherwise be ruined by mishandling, or add +1 to the potency of any potion she brews or identifies.
abilities.shortDescription: Salvages ruined potions or enhances brewed potion potency.

abilities.name: Dwarven Toughness
abilities.description: Mirelle's dwarf constitution grants her resistance to poison and advantage on saves against toxic fumes, spoiled food, or contaminated water—a necessity given her work with volatile alchemical components.
abilities.shortDescription: Poison resistance from dwarven heritage and alchemical exposure.

abilities.name: Debtor's Reckoning
abilities.description: Mirelle can refuse service to any adventurer with outstanding debts to the Guild, and can legally confiscate equipment from those who attempt to skip town without payment. She gains advantage on Intimidation checks against those who owe money.
abilities.shortDescription: Refuses service to debtors and confiscates equipment from fleeing adventurers.

item.name: Sturdy Iron Ledger
item.description: A thick-bound book with iron-reinforced covers and pages of treated vellum designed to resist water, mold, and the occasional ale spill. Its contents record every transaction made in the Supply Storeroom for the past three years, along with threatening notes about outstanding debts. Type: tool | Slot: hands | Rarity: Common | Value: 15 | Weight: 3 | Properties: Contains detailed records of all Guildhall supply transactions; pages are waterproof and tear-resistant
item.shortDescription: Waterproof ledger recording supply transactions and debts

metadata.properties: Contains detailed records of all Guildhall supply transactions; pages are waterproof and tear-resistant

item.name: Burnscar's Ring
item.description: A band of blackened iron etched with runic inscriptions that faintly shimmer when exposed to flame. Mirelle forged this ring herself after the accident that scarred her face, as a reminder and a protection against future mishaps. Type: accessory | Slot: ring | Rarity: Uncommon | Value: 220 | Weight: 0 | Status Effect: Equipper: Fire Resistance - The wearer gains resistance to fire damage and advantage on saves against effects that would cause burning. | Properties: Grants resistance to fire damage; the ring feels warm to the touch | Bonuses: constitution +2
item.shortDescription: Blackened iron ring providing protection against flames

causeStatusEffectOnEquipper.name: Fire Resistance
causeStatusEffectOnEquipper.description: Flames wash over the bearer with diminished fury, their searing touch reduced to an uncomfortable heat. Magical and mundane fire alike find it harder to take hold on skin or cloth.

item.name: Masterwork Alchemist's Kit
item.description: A well-worn leather satchel containing crystal vials, precision scales, a brass mortar and pestle, and several compartments for storing rare ingredients. The inside of the flap bears scorch marks from dozens of minor laboratory mishaps. Type: tool | Slot: waist | Rarity: Uncommon | Value: 185 | Weight: 4 | Properties: Allows crafting of potions and alchemical substances; grants advantage on checks to identify unknown liquids or compounds | Bonuses: intelligence +2
item.shortDescription: Leather satchel with vials, scales, and tools for potion crafting

metadata.properties: Allows crafting of potions and alchemical substances; grants advantage on checks to identify unknown liquids or compounds

personality.notes: She sleeps in a small room behind the secured reserves, surrounded by the smell of oil and preservation magic.
goals: Ensure the Supply Storeroom never runs out of critical items during peak expedition periods.
Repay her debt to the Cartel contact without compromising Guild security or her own position.
Identify which cursed items in the vault might be safely disassembled for components.

importantMemories: A young adventurer tried to purchase rope on credit today, and I sent him away with nothing—the look in his eyes will trouble me less than the Guild Master's reaction to another unpaid debt.
The Cartel contact left another message in my supply order, asking about Guild patrol schedules near the eastern passages.
Spent an hour after closing reorganizing the potion shelves; someone had moved the healing tinctures to the wrong section, and I will not tolerate chaos in my storeroom.

player_1772642915854_rb6f99mgj.name: Brother Osric Gault
player_1772642915854_rb6f99mgj.description: Brother Osric Gault is a broad-shouldered man in his forties, his once-powerful frame softened by years away from combat but still carrying the unconscious readiness of someone who has survived what others did not. His brown hair is thinning and shot through with premature gray, and he habitually rubs a faded scar that circles his left wrist—a reminder of shackles he has never fully explained to anyone. He wears the simple brown robes of no particular order, tending the dormitory shrine and the wounded alike with quiet competence and eyes that have seen too much to believe in easy miracles.
player_1772642915854_rb6f99mgj.shortDescription: The haunted caretaker-healer who tends the dormitory shrine.

abilities.name: Touched by the Depths
abilities.description: Osric's exposure to whatever horrors claimed his party has left him sensitive to supernatural presences. He can sense when aberrations, undead, or extraplanar beings are within thirty feet, though he cannot pinpoint their exact location.
abilities.shortDescription: Senses nearby supernatural creatures from his dungeon trauma.

abilities.name: Last Rites
abilities.description: Osric can perform a brief ritual over a dying creature that ensures their soul passes safely beyond the dungeon's reach, preventing them from rising as undead or becoming trapped in the labyrinth's cyclical curse. This takes one minute to perform.
abilities.shortDescription: Ritual that protects dying souls from dungeon corruption.

abilities.name: Wound Dust
abilities.description: Osric carries a pouch of consecrated dust that, when applied to wounds, stabilizes a dying creature and restores a small amount of health. He can use this ability three times per day before needing to recharge the dust at a shrine.
abilities.shortDescription: Consecrated dust that stabilizes and heals the dying.

abilities.name: Survivor's Guilt
abilities.description: When an ally falls in combat near Osric, he can harness his trauma-fueled focus to gain advantage on his next attack roll, ability check, or saving throw. However, each use inflicts a point of psychic damage that cannot be healed until he completes a long rest.
abilities.shortDescription: Triggers on ally death for advantage, but causes lasting psychic wounds.

item.name: Iron-Bound Healer's Kit
item.description: A heavy leather satchel reinforced with iron bands at the corners, containing bandages, needles, thread, a bone saw, tourniquets, and jars of salves and unguents with labels worn to illegibility. The kit smells of dried herbs and iron—the latter from both the bands and the bloodstains that no amount of cleaning has ever fully removed. Type: tool | Slot: hands | Rarity: Common | Value: 15 | Weight: 3 | Properties: Allows stabilization of dying characters and treatment of wounds; contains enough supplies for 10 uses before needing restocking
item.shortDescription: Leather satchel with medical supplies and tools for treating wounds

metadata.properties: Allows stabilization of dying characters and treatment of wounds; contains enough supplies for 10 uses before needing restocking

item.name: Blessed Candle Lantern
item.description: A small iron lantern with panels of thick glass, its wick fed by consecrated oil that burns with a steady amber flame regardless of wind or draft. The lantern was a gift from a dying priest who claimed it would never extinguish so long as the bearer's faith held true—a claim Osric has never tested to its limits. Type: tool | Slot: hands | Rarity: Uncommon | Value: 35 | Weight: 1.5 | Status Effect: Target: Illuminated - The target is bathed in warm, sacred light that reveals hidden truths and weakens shadow-based creatures. | Properties: Sheds light in a 30-foot radius; undead and shadow creatures make attack rolls at disadvantage within the light; never needs refueling | Bonuses: wisdom +2
item.shortDescription: Iron lantern burning consecrated oil with eternal amber flame

causeStatusEffectOnTarget.name: Illuminated
causeStatusEffectOnTarget.description: Sacred radiance clings to the target, casting no shadows and revealing that which prefers darkness. The light burns against creatures of shadow and undead, while clarifying perception and strengthening magical connection.

causeStatusEffect.name: Illuminated
causeStatusEffect.description: Sacred radiance clings to the target, casting no shadows and revealing that which prefers darkness. The light burns against creatures of shadow and undead, while clarifying perception and strengthening magical connection.

causeStatusEffectOnTarget.name: Illuminated
causeStatusEffectOnTarget.description: Sacred radiance clings to the target, casting no shadows and revealing that which prefers darkness. The light burns against creatures of shadow and undead, while clarifying perception and strengthening magical connection.

item.name: Worn Prayer Beads
item.description: A strand of fifty wooden beads carved from sanctified oak, each one smoothed by years of nervous fingers turning them in prayer or desperation. The beads hang from a braided leather cord that has been replaced more times than Osric cares to remember, and faint traces of countless blessings linger in the wood like warmth in sun-baked stone. Type: tool | Slot: accessory | Rarity: Uncommon | Value: 25 | Weight: 0.2 | Properties: Grants advantage on prayer and meditation checks; each bead can be used once per day to cast a minor blessing that heals 1d4 hit points | Bonuses: wisdom +2
item.shortDescription: Fifty sanctified oak beads on leather cord, worn smooth by years of prayer

metadata.properties: Grants advantage on prayer and meditation checks; each bead can be used once per day to cast a minor blessing that heals 1d4 hit points

item.name: Key to the Shrine Cabinet
item.description: A simple iron key worn smooth by use, hanging from a cord around Osric's neck. It opens the locked cabinet behind the dormitory shrine where he stores healing potions, holy water, and emergency supplies—things too precious or dangerous to leave unsecured in a room full of desperate strangers. Type: misc | Slot: neck | Rarity: Common | Value: 5 | Weight: 0.1 | Properties: Opens the shrine cabinet in the Guildhall dormitory; Osric guards it jealously
item.shortDescription: Simple iron key to locked shrine cabinet on cord necklace

metadata.properties: Opens the shrine cabinet in the Guildhall dormitory; Osric guards it jealously

personality.type: Neutral Good, Type 2 (Helper) with Type 4 (Individualist) undertones
personality.notes: He refuses to speak of what happened in the Abyssal Reaches, but sometimes he wakes the entire dormitory screaming in a language that no one recognizes.
goals: Keep the shrine candles burning and provide what healing his limited divine connection still allows.
Identify returning adventurers who show signs of the same corruption that claimed his former companions.
Save enough coin to commission a proper resurrection for the one party member whose body was recovered intact.

importantMemories: A young woman died in the dormitory tonight despite my prayers, and her last words were the same names I heard my own companions speak before the darkness took them.
Guild Master Venn asked me to stop recommending that adventurers avoid the eastern passages—he said my concerns were bad for business.
Found another desperate message scratched into the bedframe where I sleep, though I have no memory of carving it myself.

player_1772643077916_mb6e62p9b.name: Tobin Wheeldon
player_1772643077916_mb6e62p9b.description: Tobin Wheeldon is a wiry gnome barely three feet tall, his ink-stained fingers perpetually clutching a leather map case swollen with half-finished charts. His magnifying spectacles make his eyes appear enormous, and he has a nervous habit of adjusting them whenever conversation turns to the dangers below. He stands near the Guildhall porch each morning, hopefully approaching parties that look like they might accept a cartographer who's never actually been deeper than the Faded Halls.
player_1772643077916_mb6e62p9b.shortDescription: Nervous gnome cartographer seeking party membership.

abilities.name: Cartographer's Eye
abilities.description: After studying an area for ten minutes, Tobin can create a rough map that reveals hidden passages, secret doors, and structural weaknesses that others might miss. The map provides a bonus to Navigation checks for anyone using it within the mapped area.
abilities.shortDescription: Study area to reveal hidden passages and create navigation-boosting map.

abilities.name: Textbook Trap Sense
abilities.description: Years of studying dungeon manuals have given Tobin an intuitive sense for mechanical traps. He gains a bonus to detect pressure plates, tripwires, and similar mechanisms, though magical traps remain beyond his expertise.
abilities.shortDescription: Bonus to detect mechanical traps from extensive self-study.

abilities.name: Gnome's Quick Fingers
abilities.description: Tobin's small hands and natural dexterity allow him to manipulate small mechanisms and locks more effectively than larger folk. He can attempt to disable simple locks or mechanisms even without formal training.
abilities.shortDescription: Small hands and dexterity aid in manipulating locks and mechanisms.

item.name: Wheeldon's Partially Complete Dungeon Atlas
item.description: A bulging leather map case containing dozens of parchment sheets covered in Tobin's meticulous notation and careful cartography. The maps detail the Faded Halls in impressive detail, but grow increasingly sparse and speculative the deeper they purport to show, with entire sections labeled "rumored" or "conjectural." Type: tool | Slot: hands | Rarity: Common | Value: 25 | Weight: 3
item.shortDescription: Bulging map case with detailed charts of upper dungeon levels

item.name: Brass Magnifying Spectacles
item.description: Thick-lensed brass spectacles with adjustable arms that make Tobin's eyes appear comically large. The lenses are scratched from years of close work, but still serve their purpose of magnifying fine details for cartographic precision. Type: tool | Slot: eyewear | Rarity: Common | Value: 15 | Weight: 0.5 | Bonuses: intelligence +1
item.shortDescription: Scratched brass lenses that magnify fine details

item.name: Lucky Moth-Eaten Scarf
item.description: A faded wool scarf in what was once forest green, now more of a sickly sage after years of dungeon dampness. Tobin insists it's brought him through every expedition safely, though given he's never ventured past the Faded Halls, that's not saying much. Type: armor | Slot: neck | Rarity: Junk | Value: 2 | Weight: 0.5 | Bonuses: constitution -1
item.shortDescription: Faded damp wool scarf of questionable protective value

personality.type: Anxious ambivert, Type 5 Investigator, Neutral Good
personality.notes: He's secretly more capable than he appears, having taught himself trap detection from old texts.
goals: Create the first accurate map of the Twilight Depths and survive to publish it.
Prove to his disapproving family that cartography is a respectable and valuable profession.
Find a party willing to take on a novice cartographer before his savings run out.
Gather enough courage to actually enter the dungeon rather than just mapping the entrance area.

importantMemories: The fourth party I approached today laughed when I showed them my maps—told me they don't need a "useless paper-pusher" weighing them down.
Spent three hours at the notice board sketching the rough layout other adventurers described of the eastern Faded Halls, adding notes about the goblin scavenger movements.
The halfling pie-seller gave me a free meat pie after she saw me get rejected again; she said her son was a cartographer too, before the dungeon took him.

player_1772643077917_wcrt69zyu.name: Malphis Kethras
player_1772643077917_wcrt69zyu.description: Malphis Kethras cuts a striking figure in his dark travelling cloak, the subtle glow of his golden eyes visible even in daylight, marking his infernal heritage. His charcoal-gray skin and the small horns protruding from his swept-back white hair cause most adventurers to give him a wide berth on the porch. He waits with patient stillness, neither seeking company nor rejecting it, his patron's whispers sometimes visible as faint shadows that flicker at his shoulders.
player_1772643077917_wcrt69zyu.shortDescription: Stoic tiefling warlock with glowing eyes.

abilities.name: Pact Bolt
abilities.description: Malphis can summon a bolt of crackling dark energy from his patron's realm, hurling it at a target within medium range. The bolt deals necrotic damage and briefly flickers with shadow, making it difficult for enemies to track Malphis's position.
abilities.shortDescription: Hurl damaging shadow bolt that obscures attacker's position.

abilities.name: Whispers from Beyond
abilities.description: Malphis's patron occasionally provides fragmentary warnings about nearby dangers, supernatural phenomena, or creatures from other planes. These whispers are unreliable and cryptic, but sometimes reveal information impossible to obtain through normal means.
abilities.shortDescription: Patron occasionally warns of supernatural dangers or otherworldly creatures.

abilities.name: Infernal Resilience
abilities.description: Malphis's tiefling heritage grants him resistance to fire and necrotic damage, his body naturally absorbing and dissipating such energies. However, this tainted vitality makes him more susceptible to radiant damage, which burns him more severely than normal.
abilities.shortDescription: Resist fire and necrotic damage, but vulnerable to radiant.

abilities.name: Unsettling Presence
abilities.description: Malphis can briefly allow his patron's influence to manifest visibly, causing shadows to deepen around him and his eyes to glow more intensely. This aura unsettles enemies, imposing a penalty on their morale and concentration-based actions.
abilities.shortDescription: Deepen shadows and glow eyes to unsettle and demoralize enemies.

item.name: Pact Dagger
item.description: A ritualistic blade of darkened steel etched with infernal sigils that seem to shift when viewed from the corner of one's eye. The weapon serves as both a focus for Malphis's eldritch invocations and a conduit for his patron's power. Type: weapon | Slot: hands | Rarity: Uncommon | Value: 85 | Weight: 1.5 | Properties: Serves as a spellcasting focus; can be used to perform a minor blood ritual to recover a small amount of magical energy once per day | Bonuses: charisma +2
item.shortDescription: Ritual blade etched with shifting infernal sigils

metadata.properties: Serves as a spellcasting focus; can be used to perform a minor blood ritual to recover a small amount of magical energy once per day

item.name: Shadowweave Cloak
item.description: His dark travelling cloak is woven from fabric that seems to drink in the surrounding light, making its wearer difficult to see in dim illumination. Faint shadowy wisps occasionally drift from its hem, remnants of the fell magic used in its creation. Type: armor | Slot: back | Rarity: Uncommon | Value: 120 | Weight: 2 | Status Effect: Equipper: Shadow's Embrace - The wearer becomes slightly harder to notice in dim light or darkness, their outline blurring at the edges. | Properties: Advantage on Stealth checks in dim light or darkness | Bonuses: dexterity +2
item.shortDescription: Light-drinking cloak that blurs the wearer's outline

causeStatusEffectOnEquipper.name: Shadow's Embrace
causeStatusEffectOnEquipper.description: The wearer's form bleeds into surrounding darkness, their edges becoming indistinct and difficult to focus upon. In bright light this offers no benefit, but in shadows the effect is disconcerting to observers.

causeStatusEffect.name: Shadow's Embrace
causeStatusEffect.description: The wearer's form bleeds into surrounding darkness, their edges becoming indistinct and difficult to focus upon. In bright light this offers no benefit, but in shadows the effect is disconcerting to observers.

item.description: A simple band of aged bronze bearing an inscription in Infernal that glows faintly when its wearer channels magical energy. Malphis received this ring as part of his initial pact, a physical token of the bond between warlock and patron. Type: accessory | Slot: ring | Rarity: Uncommon | Value: 65 | Weight: 0.1 | Properties: Glow intensifies when casting warlock spells; allows the wearer to sense the general direction of their patron's influence | Bonuses: wisdom +2

These components are essential for certain invocations and rituals. personality.notes: He didn't choose his patron willingly—it appeared to him during a near-death experience and bound itself to him before he could refuse.
goals: Locate the abandoned laboratory of Valdros where his patron believes a ritual to sever pacts was being developed.
Understand the full terms of the warlock pact he entered under duress three years ago.
Find a party that will accept him despite the prejudice against tieflings and warlocks.
Survive long enough to make a decision about whether to break his pact or embrace it fully.

importantMemories: Three separate parties crossed to the other side of the porch when I approached today—the last one's cleric actually made a warding gesture at me.
My patron's whispers grew louder near the old well behind the Guildhall, suggesting something is buried beneath this very town.
I watched a group of adventurers return with a golden chalice, their faces haunted—whatever they encountered in the depths, the gold wasn't worth the cost.

player_1772642625317_c20sagwp2.name: Wanderer
player_1772642625317_c20sagwp2.description: An adventurous soul seeking fortune beneath the earth, clad in practical traveling clothes with a determined gleam in their eye.

abilities.name: Arcane Fury
abilities.description: The Wanderer channels arcane energy into their melee weapon, causing it to crackle with eldritch power for a short duration. While active, successful melee strikes deal additional magical damage and have a chance to briefly disorient opponents.
abilities.shortDescription: Imbues weapon with magic for bonus damage and disorientation

abilities.name: Untrodden Path
abilities.description: Years of wandering have honed the Wanderer's instincts for danger. They receive early warnings of ambushes and hazardous terrain, granting them improved initiative and advantage on saves against traps.
abilities.shortDescription: Grants early warning of ambushes and trap resistance

abilities.name: Desperate Deflection
abilities.description: When struck by a blow that would cause significant injury, the Wanderer can attempt to deflect part of the damage through instinctive dodge. This ability can be used once per combat encounter.
abilities.shortDescription: Reduces damage from a single heavy hit per encounter

item.name: Padded Leather Armor
item.description: Layers of boiled leather reinforced with quilted cloth at the joints, offering protection without sacrificing mobility. The straps show wear but remain functional. Type: armor | Slot: body | Rarity: Common | Value: 20 | Weight: 10 | Properties: Provides basic protection against physical attacks.
item.shortDescription: Boiled leather layers with quilted cloth reinforcement at the joints.

item.description: A reliable blade bearing the marks of honest use, its edge recently honed by a Guild weaponsmith. Type: weapon | Slot: hands | Rarity: Common | Value: 15 | Weight: 3 | Properties: A standard weapon for those who rely on strength and reach in combat. | Bonuses: strength +1, dexterity -1
item.shortDescription: A reliable used blade with a recently honed edge and worn leather grip.

player_1772643626135_gwr4ogjat.name: Wiry Gnome
player_1772643626135_gwr4ogjat.description: Wiry Gnome is a slight figure barely four feet tall, his frame all sharp angles beneath a patched leather traveling coat stained with ink and earth. Thick magnifying spectacles magnify pale grey eyes that dart nervously behind clouded lenses, and his calloused fingers constantly fret over the brass clasps of his bulging map case. He came to Dusthollow seeking to complete his mentor's unfinished survey of the Faded Halls, but three weeks of rejected party applications have left him desperate enough to approach strangers with trembling hope.
player_1772643626135_gwr4ogjat.shortDescription: Anxious gnome cartographer seeking party, clutching precious incomplete maps.

abilities.name: Memory of Stone
abilities.description: Once per dungeon level, %NAME% can mark a location in his mental map that allows him to automatically succeed on one Dungeoneering or Cartography check related to finding his way back to that location, even through shifting corridors.
abilities.shortDescription: Mark a location to guarantee finding way back once.

abilities.name: Gnome Cunning
abilities.description: %NAME% has advantage on saving throws against illusion magic and resistance to psychic damage from magical sources that attempt to confuse or disorient his senses.
abilities.shortDescription: Resist illusion magic and psychic confusion effects.

abilities.name: Thistlewick's Compass
abilities.description: The brass compass %NAME% carries grows warm when pointed toward significant unexplored chambers or hidden passages within 100 feet, though it cannot distinguish between treasure and danger.
abilities.shortDescription: Compass warms near hidden chambers or passages.

item.name: Cartographer's Field Instruments
item.description: A leather roll containing a brass compass, calibrated measuring cord, drafting pens, bottles of waterproof ink in three colors, and a small slate board for field notes. Each tool shows signs of frequent use but has been meticulously maintained. Type: tool | Slot: hands | Rarity: Common | Value: 25 | Weight: 3 | Properties: Required for creating accurate maps; can measure distances and angles in dungeon environments | Bonuses: intelligence +1
item.shortDescription: Mapping tools with compass, pens, and measuring cord

metadata.properties: Required for creating accurate maps; can measure distances and angles in dungeon environments

item.name: Stubby Iron-Shod Staff
item.description: A walking staff cut short for a small frame, its base banded with iron to grip uneven stone. The wood is worn smooth from years of nervous handling, and small notches are carved near the grip—tally marks counting something the owner prefers not to discuss. Type: weapon | Slot: hands | Rarity: Common | Value: 8 | Weight: 3 | Properties: Functions as a walking stick; iron tip useful for probing unstable ground or striking
item.shortDescription: Short walking staff with iron tip

metadata.properties: Functions as a walking stick; iron tip useful for probing unstable ground or striking

item.name: Thick Magnifying Spectacles
item.description: Clouded lenses in wire frames that magnify the wearer's eyes to comical proportions, making their pale grey irises appear enormous. The magnification is powerful enough to read faded manuscript text or examine fine details in stonework. Type: tool | Slot: eyewear | Rarity: Common | Value: 12 | Weight: 0.25 | Properties: Grants bonus to perception checks involving fine detail; slightly impairs distant vision while worn | Bonuses: wisdom +1, charisma -1
item.shortDescription: Powerful magnifying lenses for examining fine details

metadata.properties: Grants bonus to perception checks involving fine detail; slightly impairs distant vision while worn

item.name: Brass-Bound Map Case
item.description: A cylindrical case of treated leather reinforced with brass fittings at both ends, designed to keep precious documents safe from water, mold, and the dungeon's hostile environment. The clasp sticks slightly, requiring a practiced touch to open quickly. Type: container | Slot: waist | Rarity: Common | Value: 15 | Weight: 2 | Properties: Waterproof; protects documents from environmental damage
item.shortDescription: Waterproof document container with brass fittings

metadata.properties: Waterproof; protects documents from environmental damage

personality.type: INFP, Type 5 Investigator. Shy and bookish but fiercely passionate about cartography, with surprising determination beneath the anxious exterior.
personality.notes: His mentor, Master Cartographer Aldric Thistlewick, vanished in the Twilight Depths six months ago. The maps in his case are the most detailed surveys of the Faded Halls ever created—worth a fortune to the right buyer, though %NAME% refuses to sell. He carries a peculiar brass compass that always points toward the deepest part of whatever dungeon level he's on, a gift from Aldric whose true purpose remains unclear. Secretly suspects the compass is trying to lead him to his missing mentor.
goals: Complete Master Thistlewick's unfinished map of the Faded Halls.
Earn recognition from the Cartographer's Guild back in the capital.
Find a party willing to accept him before his funds run dry.
Gather enough data to publish a proper dungeon guide.

player_1772643709321_gx5y8yfn5.name: Vornath Skull-Breaker
player_1772643709321_gx5y8yfn5.description: Vornath Skull-Breaker is a heavyset half-orc with slate-gray skin and broken tusks who tends the chained ledger at The Maw with the mechanical disinterest of someone who has watched too many hopeful faces descend forever. He wears a stained Guild tabard over chainmail and keeps a crossbow within arm's reach, his small eyes constantly tracking patterns of departure and return that no one else bothers to notice. Though he speaks rarely and gruffly, his private journal contains meticulous records of which routes, party compositions, and timing correlate with survival—knowledge he trades only for favors or sufficient desperation.
player_1772643709321_gx5y8yfn5.shortDescription: Half-orc Guild clerk secretly mapping survival patterns.

abilities.name: Pattern Recognition
abilities.description: After observing a party at The Maw for a few minutes, Vornath can identify potential weaknesses in their composition or preparation based on his years of recorded data, granting advantage on checks to predict their likely survival odds.
abilities.shortDescription: Analyzes party composition to predict survival likelihood.

abilities.name: Sunken Ledger Mind
abilities.description: Vornath maintains perfect mental recall of every party that has descended through The Maw in the past year, including their stated routes, equipment, and whether they returned.
abilities.shortDescription: Perfect recall of all parties from the past year.

abilities.name: Crossbow Readiness
abilities.description: Years of watching for trouble at the checkpoint have honed Vornath's reflexes; he gains a +2 bonus to initiative and can draw and fire his crossbow as a single action.
abilities.shortDescription: Bonus to initiative and quick crossbow deployment.

item.name: Stained Guild Tabard
item.description: A once-crimson tabard now faded to dull rust, embroidered with the Adventurer's Guild crest over chainmail. Old stains from blood, mud, and cheaper ale have proven impossible to remove, giving the garment a lived-in appearance. Type: armor | Slot: body | Rarity: Common | Value: 20 | Weight: 4 | Properties: Identifies wearer as Guild-affiliated. Provides basic protection and authority at official checkpoints.
item.shortDescription: Faded Guild tabard worn over chainmail with old stains

metadata.properties: Identifies wearer as Guild-affiliated. Provides basic protection and authority at official checkpoints.

item.name: Vornath's Duty Crossbow
item.description: A sturdy, well-maintained crossbow with reinforced arms and a hair-trigger mechanism. The stock bears dozens of tally marks scratched into the wood—a grim count of warnings issued to those who threatened the checkpoint's fragile peace. Type: weapon | Slot: hands | Rarity: Common | Value: 45 | Weight: 6 | Properties: Reliable and easy to maintain. Vornath keeps it loaded but uncocked at all times. | Bonuses: dexterity +2
item.shortDescription: Sturdy crossbow marked with tallies of warnings issued

metadata.properties: Reliable and easy to maintain. Vornath keeps it loaded but uncocked at all times.

item.name: Vornath's Survivor Ledger
item.description: A leather-bound journal filled with cramped, meticulous handwriting documenting departure times, party compositions, and return statistics. Certain entries are circled in red ink, marking routes or team configurations that resulted in zero survivors. Type: tool | Slot: hands | Rarity: Uncommon | Value: 75 | Weight: 2 | Properties: Grants advantage on checks to identify safe routes through the dungeon when consulted. Contains compiled survival data that Vornath trades selectively. | Bonuses: wisdom +2, intelligence +2
item.shortDescription: Leather journal tracking dungeon survival patterns and deadly routes

importantMemories: Another party of four came back missing their cleric today—third time this month that group's lost a healer on the northeastern Faded Halls route, which means something new is hunting there.
The Guild Master asked why my ink consumption has doubled and I told him the dampness ruins the ledger, but really I have been maintaining two sets of books for months.
Watched a tiefling with a shadow-dark cloak and golden eyes descend without registering—adding him to the unlicensed list, though something about the way he moved suggests he has done this before.

player_1772643709322_0b01l9oqi.name: Ithelwen Moss-Tender
player_1772643709322_0b01l9oqi.description: Ithelwen Moss-Tender is a slender moon elf with pale silver hair bound back from a face marked by faded pockmarks from some long-ago fungal mishap, her fingers perpetually stained with bioluminescent spores. Her stall at the Last Chance Market appears disorganized—clusters of sealed glass jars, cloth-wrapped bundles, and hollowed gourds filling every surface—but she can locate any item by touch alone, and her prices rise and fall based on her private assessment of a customer's chances. She speaks in a soft, clinical monotone about growth conditions, spore behavior, and the symbiotic relationships between fungi and dungeon denizens, delivering practical warnings wrapped in terminology that sounds almost tender.
player_1772643709322_0b01l9oqi.shortDescription: Moon elf fungal merchant selling dungeon survival goods.

abilities.name: Spore Sense
abilities.description: Ithelwen can identify any fungal species by smell alone and gains advantage on checks to detect hidden fungal growths, spore traps, or creatures using fungal camouflage.
abilities.shortDescription: Identifies fungi by smell and detects fungal hazards.

abilities.name: Bioluminescent Cultivation
abilities.description: Once per day, Ithelwen can cultivate a small patch of fast-growing bioluminescent fungus that provides light equivalent to a torch for 4 hours and grows brighter when hostile creatures approach within 30 feet.
abilities.shortDescription: Creates warning fungus that glows near danger.

abilities.name: Clinical Detachment
abilities.description: Ithelwen's matter-of-fact demeanor makes her resistant to intimidation and emotional manipulation; she has advantage on saves against fear and charm effects when engaged in mercantile or research discussions.
abilities.shortDescription: Resistant to emotional manipulation during transactions.

item.name: Sporekeeper's Gloves
item.description: Thin leather gloves stained with faded streaks of blue and green, their fingertips reinforced with treated silk that allows precise handling of delicate fungal specimens without contamination. The palm contains a hidden pocket designed to hold a single dose of powdered reagent for quick application. Type: armor | Slot: gloves | Rarity: Uncommon | Value: 120 | Weight: 0.5 | Properties: Hidden palm pocket holds one dose of powdered reagent; provides resistance to contact poisons and irritating spores | Bonuses: dexterity +2, intelligence +2
item.shortDescription: Alchemist's leather gloves with hidden reagent pocket

metadata.properties: Hidden palm pocket holds one dose of powdered reagent; provides resistance to contact poisons and irritating spores

goals: Gain sufficient funding and protection to establish a research station in the Twilight Depths.
Document every fungal species in the labyrinth before the shifting corridors make them inaccessible.
Sell enough inventory to afford the specialized equipment her hybrid cultivation project requires.
Find a reliable source for Twilight Depths specimens without having to descend personally.

importantMemories: Sold three danger-sensing glowcaps to a gnome cartographer yesterday, but he seemed more interested in asking questions about my supply routes than the care instructions I was providing.
The cold snap killed half my surface cultivation stock, which means I will need to negotiate with a delver willing to harvest from the Twilight Depths fungal gardens.
A returning adventurer brought me a specimen that screamed when exposed to light—I have never seen this species before, and I am not certain it was fully dead when he handed it to me.

player_1772643929499_ij33h5ivb.name: Holt Brennan
player_1772643929499_ij33h5ivb.description: Holt Brennan is a weathered human in his fifties with iron-grey stubble and burn scars crawling up his forearms from years at the forge. His stall displays an array of secondhand blades, each weapon's history carefully omitted from his sales pitches—he buys from desperate returning adventurers and sells to desperate departing ones. His eyes hold the flat assessment of a man who has learned that questions about a blade's previous owner are bad for business.

abilities.name: Battlefield Scavenger's Eye
abilities.description: %NAME% can identify the previous owner of any weapon by examining wear patterns, nicks, and residue, learning whether it was used by a trained warrior, desperate novice, or something inhuman.
abilities.shortDescription: Identifies previous weapon owners through examination

abilities.name: Hasty Repair
abilities.description: %NAME% can temporarily restore function to a damaged weapon in minutes rather than hours, though the repair will fail catastrophically after extended combat use.
abilities.shortDescription: Quickly repairs weapons with temporary results

item.name: Reinforced Leather Gambeson
item.description: A quilted defense of boiled leather and padded cloth, stitched with iron rings at the vital points. The previous owner's initials are clumsily crossed out on the collar, and a lucky rabbit's foot still dangles from one shoulder strap. Type: armor | Slot: body | Rarity: Common | Value: 65 | Weight: 8 | Properties: Lucky charm attached, previous owner's marks crossed out
item.shortDescription: Padded leather armor with iron ring reinforcement

metadata.properties: Lucky charm attached, previous owner's marks crossed out

item.name: Notched Iron Longsword
item.description: A serviceable blade recovered from the Faded Halls, its edge marred by goblin teeth marks and what looks uncomfortably like old bloodstains that never quite scrubbed out. Holt insists the notches give it "character" and has priced it accordingly. Type: weapon | Slot: hands | Rarity: Common | Value: 35 | Weight: 3 | Properties: Bloodstained: Previous owner met a violent end
item.shortDescription: Worn blade with mysterious stains and notched edge

metadata.properties: Bloodstained: Previous owner met a violent end

personality.notes: He knows most of his weapons have blood on them already—he just prefers not to know whose.

Sell off his current inventory of questionable weapons before winter deepens.

importantMemories: Bought a notched longsword from a hollow-eyed boy who couldn't have been older than sixteen, the blade still stained with something darker than rust.
Refused to purchase a clearly cursed dagger from a tiefling merchant—the way it seemed to catch the light wrong made my skin crawl.
Heard Brunhilda's stew pot clanging and realized I hadn't eaten since yesterday; she always knows when someone needs feeding.

player_1772643929500_1za4ecx7i.name: Brunhilda Deepdelver
player_1772643929500_1za4ecx7i.description: Brunhilda Deepdelver is a stout dwarven matron whose left sleeve hangs empty, lost decades ago to a creature she refuses to discuss except to say it "tasted terrible." Her cook-fire bubbles with hearty stews and root vegetables, providing warm meals to adventurers facing the cold descent. She mothers everyone regardless of race or reputation, offering second helpings and unsolicited advice about proper layering with equal generosity.
player_1772643929500_1za4ecx7i.shortDescription: One-armed dwarf cook feeding delvers hearty meals.

player_1772643929501_9ilslrbhg.name: Magra Ironhand
player_1772643929501_9ilslrbhg.description: Magra Ironhand is a towering half-orc woman with grey-green skin and a shaved head decorated with ritual scars that trace her jawline like tribal jewelry. She wears minimal armor over her broad frame, preferring mobility when breaking up disputes between drunken adventurers and light-fingered merchants. Her judgments are swift and fair, delivered in a voice like grinding stones, and even the Guild guards defer to her authority within the market's boundaries.

player_1772643929502_1g5b7iv0k.name: Tibbit Quickwhisper
player_1772643929502_1g5b7iv0k.description: Tibbit Quickwhisper is a slight gnome with wild auburn hair and darting amber eyes perpetually scanning for listeners, his fingers twisting together in nervous compulsive gestures. He wears layers of mismatched clothing with bulging pockets full of scraps, notes, and half-remembered rumors gathered from every delver willing to share a drink. His reliability varies wildly, but occasionally his whispered intelligence proves invaluable enough to keep desperate adventurers coming back.

player_1772644212669_kmgppmrh2.name: Brakkus Heavyhand
player_1772644212669_kmgppmrh2.description: Brakkus Heavyhand is a massive half-orc with slate-gray skin and a shattered tusk, sitting motionless among the memorial stones like a statue of grief given flesh. He wears a weathered cloak over rust-spotted chainmail, his hands resting on an enormous greataxe whose blade carries deep notches from battles long past. His yellow eyes are fixed on a cluster of four markers—his former party—unmoving except when newcomers pass too close to the stones he guards.
player_1772644212669_kmgppmrh2.shortDescription: Grieving half-orc barbarian watching over fallen companions' markers.

player_1772644471016_dnsb0eb75.name: Guards
player_1772644471016_dnsb0eb75.description: Two figures in battered chainmail stand beside the warning post, breath fogging in the bitter air. The senior is a weathered human woman in her forties, gray threading through dark hair pulled back in a severe knot, her eyes carrying the flat assessment of someone who's watched too many hopeful souls descend and too few return. Beside her, a young dwarf with a patchy beard shifts his weight and tries to look authoritative, though his gaze keeps sliding toward the mist-shrouded stairs below. Guards bear the Guild's faded armbands and the particular weariness of those who maintain the boundary between the hopeful world above and the hungry dark below.
player_1772644471016_dnsb0eb75.shortDescription: Guards man the Guild's final checkpoint with practiced indifference.

abilities.name: Checkpoint Authority
abilities.description: As Guild-appointed guards, they can legally detain adventurers who refuse to provide names for the descent ledger, holding them for up to an hour without cause.
abilities.shortDescription: Can legally detain uncooperative travelers briefly

abilities.name: Jaded Watch
abilities.description: Years of observing adventurers has honed their ability to read people—Marta can often tell if someone is lying about their experience level or hiding fear behind bravado.
abilities.shortDescription: Notices when someone is bluffing about their capabilities

abilities.name: Phalanx Formation
abilities.description: When both guards fight together against a single target, they coordinate attacks to create openings, gaining an advantage on their next strike.
abilities.shortDescription: Fighting together creates coordinated attack openings

item.name: Checkpoint Lantern
item.description: A hooded iron lantern burning with oil infused to produce a steady white flame, its glass cracked but functional. The light pushes back the creeping mist from the dungeon's maw, and the hook on its base allows it to hang from the warning post's timbers during long night watches. Type: tool | Slot: hands | Rarity: Common | Value: 12 | Weight: 3
item.shortDescription: Hooded iron lantern with white flame

item.name: Battered Guild Armband
item.description: A faded crimson brassard bearing the embroidered crest of the Adventurer's Guild, its edges frayed and the fabric stiff from repeated exposure to the dungeon's damp exhalations. Worn over chainmail, it marks the bearer as an official representative of the Guild's authority—at least in theory. Type: clothing | Slot: shoulders | Rarity: Common | Value: 15 | Weight: 0.5 | Bonuses: charisma +1
item.shortDescription: Faded brassard marking Guild authority

personality.type: Pragmatic Realist, Lawful Neutral - cynical about the odds but dutiful about the job, maintains professional distance as emotional armor against the steady stream of death she witnesses
personality.notes: The senior guard, Marta, has worked the Warning Post for seven years—long enough to recognize the look in adventurers' eyes that means they won't return. She keeps a personal tally scratched into the underside of the warning post: one mark for every party that came back fewer than they left. She no longer counts the ones who don't return at all. The dwarf rookie, Thorum, is on his third month and still flinches when the mist from the depths curls particularly thick. He talks too much to cover his nerves. Marta's decided not to break him of it yet—silence makes it easier to hear the screams that sometimes echo up from the Sinking Stairs.
goals: Accumulate enough savings to request transfer to Dusthollow proper or retire from guard duty
Keep the rookie alive long enough to develop proper caution about the dungeon
Record all who pass through accurately for the Guild ledgers
Stay warm during this Veilfrost shift

The dwarf rookie, Thorum, is on his third month and still flinches when the mist from the depths curls particularly thick. He talks too much to cover his nerves. Marta's decided not to break him of it yet—silence makes it easier to hear the screams that sometimes echo up from the Sinking Stairs.
goals: Accumulate enough savings to request transfer to Dusthollow proper or retire from guard duty
Keep the rookie alive long enough to develop proper caution about the dungeon
Record all who pass through accurately for the Guild ledgers
Stay warm during this Veilfrost shift

player_1772644565902_036vcsm1r.name: Brannick Holt
player_1772644565902_036vcsm1r.description: Brannick Holt is a weathered human in his mid-forties whose stubbled face bears the permanent squint of a man who has watched too many hopeful adventurers descend and too few return. His chainmail is polished but his eyes are dull, and he passes the time tossing dice with his fellow guard or nursing a wineskin that never seems to empty. Beneath his bored exterior lies a careful intelligence—he knows exactly which descending parties to report to his Cartel contacts and which to leave alone.
player_1772644565902_036vcsm1r.shortDescription: Weary Guild guard with secret Cartel ties

abilities.name: Checkpoint Authority
abilities.description: Brannick can demand to see expedition papers and delay parties for up to an hour with bureaucratic questions, useful for stalling competitors or buying time for Cartel associates to position themselves.
abilities.shortDescription: Can delay parties with official document checks and questions

abilities.name: Eyes Forward
abilities.description: Years of watching the descent have taught Brannick to read adventurers' body language; he can accurately gauge a party's experience level and general morale with a brief observation.
abilities.shortDescription: Gauges party experience and morale through observation

abilities.name: Cartel Whisper
abilities.description: Once per day, Brannick can pass a coded message to Cartel operatives among descending parties, alerting them to opportunities or warnings without drawing attention.
abilities.shortDescription: Sends coded messages to Cartel allies in descending parties

item.name: Guard's Spear
item.description: A sturdy iron-tipped spear with a ash haft worn smooth from years of handling. The weapon is practical rather than decorative, designed to keep trouble at bay rather than for heroic combat. Type: weapon | Slot: hands | Rarity: Common | Value: 15 | Weight: 6 | Bonuses: strength +1
item.shortDescription: Well-maintained iron-tipped spear with worn ash haft

item.name: Cartel Signal Whistle
item.description: A small silver whistle on a chain, seemingly innocent but bearing tiny etchings that identify the bearer to the Dusthollow Cartel's agents. Three short bursts signals a profitable opportunity; one long blast warns of Guild investigators nearby. Type: tool | Slot: accessory | Rarity: Uncommon | Value: 25 | Weight: 0 | Bonuses: charisma +2
item.shortDescription: Silver whistle with secret Cartel signal codes

personality.type: Lawful Neutral, Enneagram Type 6 (Loyalist). Not blindly loyal to institutions but deeply loyal to people who've earned his trust; his cynicism masks genuine concern for those he watches descend.
personality.notes: He keeps a small leather journal where he records names of everyone who passes, adding checkmarks when they return
goals: Accumulate enough coin to retire somewhere far from Dusthollow
Break free from Cartel obligations without getting killed
Get through this week's shift without any more deaths on his conscience
Identify which adventuring parties are Cartel plants versus genuine hopefuls

importantMemories: A Cartel runner approached me behind the shelter yesterday with another pouch of coin and questions about the gnome cartographer's schedule—told him Thistlewick's been gone three weeks now.
Watched a party of four descend at dawn; recognized one as the blacksmith's daughter from Dusthollow and marked her name in my journal with a silent prayer.
The new guard, Pentrick, asked too many questions about my extra coin so I told him my aunt died and left me a small inheritance—don't think he believed me.

player_1772644565903_sd00xx9lw.name: Sycorax Stone-Etcher
player_1772644565903_sd00xx9lw.description: Sycorax Stone-Etcher is a tiefling woman with slate-grey skin and curved horns wrapped in copper wire, her amber eyes perpetually fixed on measurements, sketches, and celestial charts. She carries a case bulging with parchment and measuring instruments, and speaks rapidly when engaged, often forgetting to let others respond. Her obsession with proving the Sinking Stairs move according to lunar phases has made her a familiar sight at the Warning Post, where she sketches for hours regardless of the cold.
player_1772644565903_sd00xx9lw.shortDescription: Obsessive tiefling scholar mapping the stairs

abilities.name: Lunar Cartography
abilities.description: Thessara can predict the general stability of dungeon passages based on current moon phase, granting advantage on navigation checks in the Faded Halls during favorable lunar conditions.
abilities.shortDescription: Predicts dungeon stability based on moon phases

abilities.name: Obsessive Measurement
abilities.description: When Thessara has time to study an area for at least ten minutes, she creates detailed measurements that provide bonuses to detecting secret doors, hidden compartments, or architectural anomalies.
abilities.shortDescription: Detailed study reveals hidden architectural features

abilities.name: Theoretical Knowledge
abilities.description: Thessara's extensive reading on the dungeon's history allows her to recall obscure facts about Valdros's citadel, its original layout, and the magical catastrophe that created the labyrinth.
abilities.shortDescription: Recalls obscure facts about dungeon history and layout

item.name: Scholar's Traveling Robes
item.description: Heavy wool robes dyed a deep midnight blue, patched at the elbows and hemmed short to avoid trailing on wet stone. Numerous inner pockets hold vials of ink, spare quills, and wrapped rations, while a cleverly concealed pocket near the heart can secure small valuables. Type: armor | Slot: body | Rarity: Common | Value: 28 | Weight: 4 | Properties: Provides minor protection against cold and damp. The many pockets allow easy access to writing implements and small tools without searching through a pack.
item.shortDescription: Patched midnight blue robes with numerous inner pockets

metadata.properties: Provides minor protection against cold and damp. The many pockets allow easy access to writing implements and small tools without searching through a pack.

item.name: Valdros Surveyor's Case
item.description: A battered leather case reinforced with bronze corners, containing telescoping measuring rods, calibrated chains, and a precision astrolabe. The interior lining is stained with ink from a long-ago spill, and several compartments hold chalk sticks and charcoal for marking corridor walls. Type: tool | Slot: hands | Rarity: Uncommon | Value: 85 | Weight: 6 | Properties: Grants advantage on checks to map passages, detect architectural anomalies, and track movement patterns within the labyrinth. The telescoping rods can measure distances up to 30 feet. | Bonuses: intelligence +2, wisdom +2
item.shortDescription: Leather case with surveying instruments for dungeon mapping

item.name: Copper-Wrapped Research Journal
item.description: A thick-bound journal wrapped in protective oilcloth, its covers reinforced with thin copper plates etched with lunar phase diagrams. Hundreds of pages are filled with cramped handwriting, architectural sketches, celestial calculations, and increasingly obsessive marginalia about the Sinking Stairs. Type: tool | Slot: hands | Rarity: Common | Value: 15 | Weight: 2 | Properties: Contains Sycorax's accumulated research on lunar-correlated passage shifts. A knowledgeable reader might glean useful navigation insights, though the disorganized notes require patience to interpret.
item.shortDescription: Scholar's journal with lunar phase research and corridor sketches

metadata.properties: Contains Sycorax's accumulated research on lunar-correlated passage shifts. A knowledgeable reader might glean useful navigation insights, though the disorganized notes require patience to interpret.

item.name: Lunar-Phase Astrolabe
item.description: A palm-sized brass astrolabe engraved with three overlapping dial faces representing the moon's phases, the dungeon's tidal magical fluctuations, and something called the "Deep Rhythm" in faded gilt lettering. A small crystal lens focuses ambient light onto the calibration marks. Type: accessory | Slot: accessory | Rarity: Uncommon | Value: 120 | Weight: 1 | Properties: Once per day, can determine the current lunar phase and predict which stair configurations are most likely in the Sinking Stairs. The crystal lens can detect faint magical residues within 10 feet. | Bonuses: wisdom +3
item.shortDescription: Brass astronomical instrument for tracking lunar phases and dungeon shifts

personality.notes: She has named individual steps of the Sinking Stairs and refers to them by number and their position relative to "Stella"
goals: Document enough stair-shift patterns to publish her findings
Secure funding for a proper cartographic expedition into the Faded Halls
Finish her current sketch before her fingers go numb from cold
Find someone willing to trade astronomical observations for food

importantMemories: Step 47 was definitely two inches further east today compared to yesterday's measurements, confirming my hypothesis that the waning moon causes outward expansion.
The guards have stopped asking why I'm here again; the one with the sad eyes even shared his wineskin with me yesterday while I was explaining stellar parallax.
A returning adventurer told me the Faded Halls shifted during the new moon and I nearly dropped my measuring calipers in excitement—this changes everything about my projections.

player_1772644953014_0psyfa4dx.name: Hollow Sentinel Valdrus
player_1772644953014_0psyfa4dx.description: Hollow Sentinel Valdrus is the animated skeleton of an imperial guardsman, clad in chainmail so corroded it flakes at the edges, bearing the illegible remnants of a once-proud crest. It patrols a section of the Sinking Stairs with mechanical precision, attacking any who descend without speaking the passphrase that died with its empire centuries ago. Pinpricks of baleful green light glow in its empty eye sockets, and it wields a notched longsword with surprising skill despite its skeletal form.
player_1772644953014_0psyfa4dx.shortDescription: Undead imperial guard eternally patrolling the Sinking Stairs.

abilities.name: Eternal Vigil
abilities.description: Passive: The sentinel does not tire, sleep, or stop patrolling. It gains advantage on perception checks to detect approaching creatures and cannot be surprised while on its designated patrol route.
abilities.shortDescription: Never tires, detects approaching creatures with supernatural awareness

abilities.name: Rusted Blade Strike
abilities.description: Active: The sentinel's notched longsword swings with mechanical precision. On a successful hit, the target must succeed a constitution save or contract minor tetanus, suffering disadvantage on strength checks for the next hour.
abilities.shortDescription: Sword strikes can inflict lingering weakness through infection

abilities.name: Passphrase Demand
abilities.description: Triggered: Before initiating combat, the skeleton halts and rasps a single demanding phrase in Old Imperial. Creatures who respond in the same ancient tongue gain one round to act before it attacks.
abilities.shortDescription: Pauses before combat to demand ancient password

item.name: Flaking Chainmail Hauberk
item.description: Rusted links that shed orange flakes with every movement, this armor once protected an imperial guardsman. Though corroded, the mail still turns aside weaker blows and carries lingering protective enchantments. Type: armor | Slot: body | Rarity: Common | Value: 30 | Weight: 25 | Bonuses: constitution +1, dexterity -1
item.shortDescription: Rusted chainmail with fading protective magic

item.name: Corroded Imperial Longsword
item.description: A notched blade bearing the faint etching of a sunburst crest, its edge dulled by centuries yet still capable of cleaving flesh. The metal has darkened with age and something darker seems to cling to the steel, whispering faintly when drawn. Type: weapon | Slot: hands | Rarity: Uncommon | Value: 45 | Weight: 3 | Status Effect: Target: Necrotic Chill - The wound frosts over and the flesh around it turns gray and numb, slowing movement and dulling reflexes. | Bonuses: strength +2, dexterity -2
item.shortDescription: Notched ancient blade that numbs wounded flesh

importantMemories: The passphrase was something about stars, I remember that much—ordered to let no one pass without it, and the order still burns in what remains of my mind.
Watched the citadel sink through eyes that were still flesh, heard the screaming, felt the magic tear through me before the cold took everything but duty.
A woman descended yesterday speaking soft words I almost recognized, and for a moment the sword felt heavy in my hand—then the mist closed in and the moment passed.
